ab: laqi cu na ga, mnaki cami qalang Skikun, qaring cu mqwas biru lga, musa cami maki Girang la, ski mwani mqyanux cami cqa heci.
ch: 小時候我們家住在四季，上學以後，就搬到了宜蘭，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Skikun. After I started school, we moved to Yilan and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: mqwas cu raygako lga, musa cu tqbaq gaga qnxan na Tayal la, mica kbqun mu qabax mnanaq gaga na qnxan Genzuming.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: mica qa, ryax hngawan bagan ga, kingan ru qbsuyal mu mlikuy musa cami mkan bnheci cqa qalang, smoya cu calay tqbaq gaga na glux mu nanaq.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: ktalan mu bnheci qani ga spatun mpux kawas nya la, ana ga pisan hayi sening ktalan na.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: tkbaqan mu, baq kmayan ke nanu qa hiya, bbaq ru hyuci calay knayan nya ke Tayal, baq kmayan ke kahat ru Truko ru ke Gipun uyi.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的泰雅語說得很流利，也會說閩南語、太魯閣語和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Atayal, as well as Hokkien, Truku and Japanese.

ab: tqbaqan mu loyi ga. mica kbaqun mu maha, baq tgaring llaqi na ga, tqbaq ke Gipun, kneril nya kahul Truko ru, mqbaq piyux kahat cqa ptyawan nya.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是太魯閣族人，而他工作上認識許多閩南人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Truku, and he knows many Hokkien at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: mkkayan cami ki bnheci qani ga, piyux wal nya pinqiyan kinbaqan na qmalup qani.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: ana myan ini kbaqi ciyan mung pinqiyan nya ga, wal myan halan ppsyasing ke pinqiyan nya, ana myan blequn mung qa ke nya loyi.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: llaqi cu na ga, ini mu kbaqi ciyan gaga na glux mu nanaq, mica mcayux cu hayi son ma cquliq cu Tayal.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: tqbaqan mu gaga qani lga, mqas cu calay son ma Tayal, mnanaq ru strahu mu ru ssyun mu calay inlungan.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為泰雅族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Tayal is unique and something to be proud of.
